_comment: Hand-curated cross-game sound relationships. Used by the Genealogy panel (Step 5.4) to populate A/B diff with related-pair picks. 'family' groups identical or near-identical implementations across games; 'kinship' notes algorithmic descent (same engine, different parameters).

families.name: Lightning (LFSR)
families.notes: Defender + Stargate share the LITE engine byte-for-byte (modulo the Stargate IRQ-stub probe). Robotron's APPEAR ($12) re-uses the same LFSR routine with a different LFREQ seed.

families.name: Heartbeat (GWAVE)
families.notes: Heart-beat divorted-from-vermin. Both games use the same wavetable + WVDECA decay structure.

families.name: Variable-duty saw (VARI)
families.notes: Descending-pitch square wave produced by alternating LOCNT/HICNT countdowns with per-sweep LODT/HIDT deltas.

families.name: Scream (SCREAM)
families.notes: 4 detuned voices summed at the DAC, each with independently-decaying FREQ/TIMER. Present in all three games — born on Defender (whose game CPU never triggers it in play); Robotron adds per-voice seed-detune on voice spawn for the swarm-of-echoes effect.

families.name: Organ (ORGAN)
families.notes: Polyphonic via OSCIL bitmask popcount; the RDELAY scratchpad mutates per tune step. In all three games — Defender plays PHANTOM / TACCATA, Stargate & Robotron play Fifth / Ninth (the famous wave-start jingle). Stargate's ORGANN ($1C) is gutted to a no-op stub.

_format_notes: members[].cmd is the DECIMAL command code (so $1A = 26). The A/B panel converts decimal → hex display on the way in.
